content.text:
{"approved": true, "feedback": "All 10 requirements from the task are correctly implemented in src/app.ts:

1. ✓ Imports updated to include `clearSearch` from toolbar-handlers.js
2. ✓ `searchBtn` element creation entirely removed (grep confirms zero references in app.ts)
3. ✓ `searchWrapper` div with `searchIcon` (bi bi-search search-icon), `searchClearBtn` button with `clearIcon` (bi bi-x-lg), correct `aria-label`, and correct append ORDER (input, icon, clear-btn) matching the spec
4. ✓ `toolbar.append(breadcrumbEl, searchWrapper, uploadLabel)` — searchInput/searchBtn replaced with searchWrapper
5. ✓ `searchBtn.addEventListener('click', doSearch)` removed
6. ✓ Debounced input listener with 200ms timer, typed via `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>`, null-guards correctly, resets timer to null inside the timeout callback
7. ✓ `searchClearBtn.addEventListener('click', clearSearch)` present
8. ✓ keydown listener handles both Enter (doSearch) and Escape (stopPropagation + clearSearch); the stopPropagation rationale is documented in a comment
9. ✓ `DomRefs` object and `init(refs)` call contain ONLY results, status, breadcrumb, searchInput, uploadInput — confirmed searchClearBtn/searchWrapper are NOT added; also verified context.ts DomRefs interface is unchanged
10. ✓ Module doc comment updated: toolbar structure now reads `.search-wrapper (search <input> + search-icon + clear-btn) + <label> upload` — no mention of a search button

Scope discipline: dialog, trigger, close button, upload label/input, and widget structure are all unchanged.

Quality:
- `clearSearch` is correctly exported from toolbar-handlers.js (verified source)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly with no errors
- No dead code, no leftover debug statements, no commented-out code, no stale TODOs
- Comments are accurate and explain the WHY (CSS sibling-combinator ordering, dialog ESC stopPropagation, why no ref needed for clear-btn)
- The file is internally consistent and the doc comment matches the new DOM structure

The expected test failures in start-app.test.ts / toolbar-handlers.test.ts are explicitly out of scope per the task (updated in subsequent tasks), so they are not flagged.", "issues": []}
